title: Five Challah Shapes and What They Symbolize
title_zh: 五种辫子面包的形状及其象征意义
description: Round for Rosh Hashana, braided for Shabbat, key-shaped for after Pesach — learn the shapes, their meanings, and how to form each one.
cultural_parallels: Chinese knot-tying (中国结) and braiding as symbols of unity
Cyclical time in Chinese philosophy and Jewish calendar
Red envelopes and symbolic foods for blessing and prosperity
Phoenix crown (凤冠) and crown-shaped challah as symbols of honor

historical_periods.chinese_equivalent: Warring States through Han Dynasty (战国时代至汉朝)

key_concepts: Three-strand braid: Unity of patriarchs or truth/peace/justice
Six-strand braid: Six days of work or six orders of Mishnah
Round challah: Cyclical time and God's crown during High Holidays
Ladder challah: Prayers ascending to heaven (Jacob's ladder)
Key challah: Opening gates of divine sustenance after Passover
Crown challah: Royalty and honor for celebrations
Hafrashat challah: The mitzvah of separating a portion of dough
practical_instructions: Key shape with shaft and teeth
